response:
[SOPHIA HAS TWO SCIENCE TESTS, BIOLOGY AND CHEMISTRY, WITH 25 AND 20 QUESTIONS, RESPECTIVELY. SHE HAS 45 MINUTES TO COMPLETE THE BIOLOGY TEST AND 1 HOUR TO COMPLETE THE CHEMISTRY TEST. IF SOPHIA WANTS TO ALLOCATE THE SAME AMOUNT OF TIME FOR EACH QUESTION, HOW MANY MORE SECONDS CAN SHE SPEND ON EACH QUESTION IN THE CHEMISTRY TEST COMPARED TO THE BIOLOGY TEST?]

[CONVERT THE TIME FOR EACH TEST INTO SECONDS. FOR THE BIOLOGY TEST, 45 MINUTES IS 2700 SECONDS. DIVIDE BY 25 QUESTIONS TO GET 108 SECONDS PER QUESTION. FOR THE CHEMISTRY TEST, 1 HOUR IS 3600 SECONDS. DIVIDE BY 20 QUESTIONS TO GET 180 SECONDS PER QUESTION. SUBTRACT 108 FROM 180 TO FIND THE DIFFERENCE. THE DIFFERENCE IS 72 SECONDS. SOPHIA CAN SPEND 72 MORE SECONDS ON EACH QUESTION IN THE CHEMISTRY TEST COMPARED TO THE BIOLOGY TEST.]
